steps.title: Target the universal claim
steps.content: The claim says egocentric temporal reference is common across all human groups, so the Yupno also use a self-centered frame of reference for time. To undermine that claim, the finding should show that the Yupno's time gestures are not based on the speaker's own body-centered left, right, front, or back.

steps.title: Reject body-centered evidence
steps.content: Choice A still uses left and right directions when discussing future and past, even if the hand use differs from the example. Choice B also keeps time tied to left-right pointing from the person. Choice D replaces pointing with head movement, but the directions are still left and right relative to the body.

steps.title: Use the terrain-based pattern
steps.content: Choice C makes the gesture depend on orientation to the hill: with backs toward the top of a hill, the Yupno point backward for the future, but when facing the top of a hill, they point to the front. That landmark-based pattern directly undermines an egocentric account. The answer is C.
